mission.title: fluent-bit: DNS resolution timeout/failure in >= 1.8.5
mission.description: DNS resolution timeout/failure in >= 1.8.5. This issue affects 20+ users.

steps.title: Identify fluent-bit troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your fluent-bit deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit -n fluent-bit --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review fluent-bit configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant fluent-bit configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit
kubectl get configmap -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=fluent-bit
```
Hi, I am facing a DNS resolution timeout/failure since upgrading to >= 1.8.5 with the forward module to a fluentd instance. It is working fine with 1.8.4. I am running on ubuntu 20.04 and the local resolver accept UDP and TCP requests.

steps.title: Apply the fix for DNS resolution timeout/failure in >= 1.8.5
steps.description:
Hey folks, here is the results of my repro attempts. I was able to confirm this issue report, at least for the datadog output. 

#### Base Config

I tested 4 different outputs:
* cloudwatch_logs
* es (sending to Amazon OpenSearch, and I also sourced credentials from the STS API, so two AWS service
```yaml
[2021/09/03 11:27:49] [ info] [engine] started (pid=2320367)
[2021/09/03 11:27:49] [ info] [storage] version=1.1.1, initializing...
[2021/09/03 11:27:49] [ info] [storage] root path '/var/td-agent-bit/storage'
[2021/09/03 11:27:49] [ info] [storage] normal synchronization mode, checksum disabled, max_chunks_up=128
[2021/09/03 11:27:49] [ info] [storage] backlog input plugin: storage_backlog.2
[2021/09/03 11:27:49] [ info] [cmetrics] version=0.2.1
[2021/09/03 11:27:49] [ info] [input:storage_backlog:storage_backlog.2] queue memory limit: 95.4M
[2021/09/03 11:27:55] [ info] [http_server] listen 
```

steps.title: Upgrade fluent-bit to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade fluent-bit:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade fluent-bit fluent-bit/fluent-bit --namespace fluent-bit
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n fluent-bit
helm list -n fluent-bit
```

steps.title: Confirm DNS resolution timeout/failure in >= 1.8.5 is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit -n fluent-bit --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n fluent-bit --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: Hey folks, here is the results of my repro attempts. I was able to confirm this issue report, at least for the datadog output.
codeSnippets:
[2021/09/03 11:27:49] [ info] [engine] started (pid=2320367)
[2021/09/03 11:27:49] [ info] [storage] version=1.1.1, initializing...
[2021/09/03 11:27:49] [ info] [storage] root path '/var/td-agent-bit/storage'

[2021/09/03 11:27:49] [ info] [storage] backlog input plugin: storage_backlog.2
[2021/09/03 11:27:49] [ info] [cmetrics] version=0.2.1

[2021/09/03 11:27:55] [ info] [http_server] listen iface=127.0.0.1 tcp_port=2020
[2021/09/03 11:27:55] [ info] [sp] stream processor started
[2021/09/03 11:27:55] [ info] [input:tail:tail_proftpd_log] inotify_fs_add(): inode=516227 watch_fd=1 name=/va

# Output all logs to fluentd instances
[OUTPUT]
    Name forward
    Alias forward_to_fluentd
    Match das.scanner.*
    Upstream upstream.conf
    Retry_Limit False
    tls on
[UPSTREAM]
    name    forward-balancing
[NODE]
    name    fluentd
    host    fluentd.example.org
    port    24224
    tls     on

[INPUT]
    Name dummy
    Tag dummy

[OUTPUT]
    Name datadog
    Match *
    Host http-intake.logs.datadoghq.com
    TLS On
    apikey  REDACTED
    dd_service my-test-service-dns-issue
    dd_source fluent-bit
    dd_tags project:example
    provider ecs


[OUTPUT]
    Name  http
    Match *
    Host  google.com
    Port  80
    URI   /

[OUTPUT]
    Name  es
    Match *
    Host  REDACTED
    Port  443
    Index my_index
    Type  my_type
    AWS_Auth On
    AWS_Region us-west-2
    tls     On
    AWS_Role_Arn REDACTED

[OUTPUT]
    Name cloudwatch_logs
    Match   *
    region us-east-1
    log_group_name fluent-bit-cloudwatch
    log_stream_prefix from-fluent-bit-
    auto_create_group On
    net.dns.mode TCP

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with fluent-bit installed or the issue environment reproducible.
